name: CourtListener Monitor
failure_modes: 401 — token missing or expired. Register at courtlistener.com/sign-in/
0 results — civil rights query terms may need updating
400 unknown_params — endpoint changed, check courtlistener.com/api/
Slow — page_size safely increase to 50
notes: Primary legal source. Searches section 1983, excessive force, civil rights, police brutality. Federal cases only.

name: AP RSS Monitor
failure_modes: 0 results — scope filter may be too restrictive, check _IN_SCOPE_TERMS
Feed URL changed — check apnews.com for current RSS paths
Connection timeout — AP occasionally throttles, add retry logic
notes: Breaking news source. Filters for police brutality, hate crimes, civil rights. Scope filter critical — must not pull off-topic records.

name: DOJ Press Monitor
failure_modes: 0 results — DOJ filter terms may not match current press release language
Feed URL changed — check justice.gov/news for current RSS
Returns non-civil-rights content — tighten _DOJ_CIVIL_RIGHTS filter terms
notes: Federal civil rights prosecutions. No key required. Filters for civil rights, police misconduct, excessive force, color of law, hate crime.

name: Fatal Encounters Monitor
failure_modes: Site down — check fatalencounters.org directly
Google Sheet moved — search for Fatal Encounters dataset on Google
CSV format changed — update import script column mappings
notes: PENDING IMPORT. 30,000+ police killing records since 2000. Download as CSV from Google Sheet. Run import_fatal_encounters.py once downloaded. Will be largest single source.

name: Mapping Police Violence Monitor
failure_modes: Download page moved — check mappingpoliceviolence.us directly
XLSX format changed — update import script column mappings
Duplicate records with Fatal Encounters — deduplicate by victim name + date + state
notes: PENDING IMPORT. Most comprehensive police killing dataset since 2013. XLSX download. Run import_mpv.py once downloaded. Includes race, age, armed status, charges filed.

name: EJI Data Monitor
failure_modes: No public API — manual import only
Report URLs change annually — check eji.org for current reports
PDF format — requires extraction, not direct parse
notes: PENDING IMPORT. Equal Justice Initiative. Nearly 6,500 documented lynchings 1865-1950. Manual import from public reports. Historical records — all is_historical=True.

name: Sovereign Bodies Monitor
failure_modes: No public API yet — CSV download only
Data requires tribal verification — handle with care
Some records confidential by request of families — respect and do not publish
notes: PENDING IMPORT. MMIW/MMIP database from 1900 to present. Most comprehensive Indigenous missing and murdered dataset. Handle with reverence — these are real people, real families.

name: ProPublica Monitor
failure_modes: Feed URL changed — check propublica.org for current RSS
0 civil rights results — add propublica.org to Obasi TRUSTED_DOMAINS
Paywalled articles — summary only, link to full article
notes: RECOMMENDED — not yet active. Investigative civil rights reporting. No key required. Add feed URL to scanner AP_FEEDS list.

name: Indian Country Today Monitor
failure_modes: Feed URL changed — check ictnews.org for current RSS
0 MMIW results — add mmiw keywords to scope filter
Domain not trusted — add ictnews.org to Obasi TRUSTED_DOMAINS
notes: RECOMMENDED — not yet active. Indigenous press. Critical for MMIW coverage. No key required. Add ictnews.org to Obasi TRUSTED_DOMAINS first.

name: Geographic Cluster Analyst
notes: Zero records in expected high-density states signals source gap, not absence of violence. Flag to Ash for source recommendations. Flag to Drum for analysis report.

name: Documentation Gap Analyst
known_gaps: MMIW cases: federal database has 116 of 5,712 reported — gap is the story
Police killings pre-2000: no comprehensive database exists
Lynchings outside South: Northern and Western racial terror underreported
Hate crimes: 87% of US jurisdictions do not report to FBI UCR
Prison deaths: not tracked as police killings despite custody
notes: Documentation gaps are not neutral. They are themselves evidence of systemic erasure. Drum should surface these in analysis reports.

name: Temporal Pattern Analyst
known_patterns: Police killings spike in summer months — more outdoor activity, more encounters
Hate crimes spike after major political events — elections, court decisions
MMIW cases cluster around highways and truck routes — known trafficking corridors
Lynchings clustered around harvest season — economic competition with Black labor
Post-Reconstruction violence peaked 1877-1950 — end of federal protection
notes: Temporal patterns provide context for clusters. Drum should include in analysis. Flag unusual spikes to River for watchdog review.
